title: OpenClaw v2026.5.26: Faster Gateway, Security & Reaction Approvals
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.26 ships cached gateway startup, SSRF+prompt-injection security hardening, emoji reaction approvals on WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage, and OpenTelemetry observability.
author: PulseAgent
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.26: Faster Gateway, Security Hardening & Reaction Approvals

*Released May 27, 2026 · 100+ contributors*

OpenClaw v2026.5.26 delivers three headline improvements for production B2B deployments: a gateway startup optimization that eliminates redundant scans, a multi-layer security model that blocks SSRF and prompt-injection attacks, and reaction-based channel approvals that let operators approve agent actions with a single emoji tap.

## What's New in v2026.5.26

### 1. Gateway Startup Optimization

The gateway now caches plugin metadata, package paths, and session data on first load — eliminating repeated plugin/channel/session re-discovery on every restart. For deployments running dozens of plugins or connecting to multiple sales channels simultaneously, this means faster cold-start recovery and more reliable uptime during rolling restarts.

> **Key fact:** Plugin/channel/session re-discovery overhead is now paid once, not on every gateway restart — a significant improvement for high-availability deployments.

### 2. Security Hardening: SSRF + Prompt Injection Prevention

v2026.5.26 introduces a layered security model that enterprise B2B deployments have needed:

- **SSRF policy enforcement**: Browser snapshots now honor your configured SSRF policy, preventing agents from being leveraged to probe internal infrastructure.
- **Prompt injection prevention**: System-event text is sanitized before entering the LLM context, closing a class of injection attacks where external content could impersonate system instructions.
- **External content boundaries**: Fetched file text is wrapped as clearly-labeled external content — models always distinguish trusted system instructions from untrusted external input.
- **Stale device token rejection**: Push notification tokens are validated at send time; deregistered device tokens are dropped cleanly rather than accumulating silently.
- **ClickClack sender allowlists**: Enforced sender verification on the ClickClack channel prevents unauthorized message injection.
- **Tool-call serialization scrubbed**: Tool-call metadata is scrubbed from outbound replies to prevent information leakage.

For B2B deployments handling sensitive CRM data and prospect communications, this security layer substantially reduces your attack surface.

### 3. Reaction-Based Channel Approvals

Signal, iMessage, and WhatsApp now support **reaction-based approvals** — agents can present a message with reaction options, and operators approve or deny by reacting rather than typing a command.

This is a meaningful UX upgrade for sales operations: instead of requiring your sales manager to type "approve" in a thread, they tap a thumbs-up emoji. The approval is logged, traceable, and requires no separate dashboard.

**Channels supporting reaction approvals:**
- Signal
- iMessage
- WhatsApp
- Telegram: typing/progress context preserved; forum topic threading maintained
- Discord: voice playback improved

### 4. Realtime Talk: Inspectable, Steerable & Cancellable

The Realtime Talk engine is now fully controllable from the Web UI and Discord:

- **Inspectable**: See current voice session state in real time
- **Steerable**: Redirect agent focus mid-conversation from the dashboard
- **Cancellable**: Kill a runaway voice session without restarting the gateway

Wake-name handling is more tolerant of ambient speech — partial name matches in a busy office or call center no longer trigger the agent. Shared realtime turn-context tracking is synchronized across all voice SDKs for consistent cross-channel state.

### 5. Observability: Activity Tab + OpenTelemetry LLM Spans

A new **Activity tab** in the Web UI surfaces sanitized summaries of tool activity — what ran, what was blocked, and why. This is the single-pane-of-glass view that sales ops teams have wanted for auditing agent behavior.

OpenTelemetry LLM spans are now emitted for gateway operations, giving your Datadog, Grafana, or Honeycomb stack direct visibility into model call latency and error rates.

New telemetry signals in this release:
- Blocked tool events with reason codes
- Failover events with context
- Stale session warnings
- Gateway secret-prep traces

### 6. Transcript-Backed Architecture

Meeting summaries and media provenance are now consolidated in a unified transcript-backed store. This is the infrastructure layer for **meeting-aware SDR**: an agent that attended a discovery call can now reference that transcript when crafting a follow-up message, without manual copy-paste.

### 7. Image Processing: Sharp → Rastermill

Image processing migrates from Sharp to Rastermill. For deployments processing attachments, screenshots, or product images in WhatsApp/iMessage threads, Rastermill provides more predictable memory behavior and full Alpine Linux compatibility — important for containerized production deployments.

---

## Upgrade Impact for B2B SDR Deployments

| Feature | Before v2026.5.26 | After v2026.5.26 |
|---|---|---|
| Gateway restart overhead | Re-scans all plugins/sessions every time | Cached — one-time scan |
| Channel approvals | Text commands required (approve) | Emoji reactions work |
| SSRF protection | Not enforced on browser snapshots | Enforced by policy |
| Prompt injection risk | System events could be spoofed | Sanitized before LLM context |
| Tool visibility | No built-in audit view | Activity tab + OTel spans |
| Voice session control | Limited Web UI control | Inspectable, steerable, cancellable |
| Meeting follow-ups | Manual transcript lookup | Transcript-backed store |

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Do reaction approvals work in WhatsApp group chats?**
A: Yes. On WhatsApp and iMessage, reaction-based approvals work in both 1:1 and group contexts. The approval is attributed to the reacting user.

**Q: Do I need to update my agent config to get the security improvements?**
A: No. SSRF policy enforcement and prompt injection sanitization are automatic. ClickClack sender allowlists require a one-time config entry only if you use that channel.

**Q: Will the Sharp → Rastermill migration break my existing image workflows?**
A: No. Rastermill is API-compatible with Sharp. Deployments on Alpine Linux will see the most improvement.

**Q: What does "transcript-backed architecture" mean for my SDR team?**
A: Meeting summaries are now stored in a persistent, queryable store. Your agents can reference past calls when writing follow-ups — this is the infrastructure that enables meeting-aware personalization.

**Q: Where do I see what tools my agents are running?**
A: Open the Activity tab in the OpenClaw Web UI. It shows a sanitized log of tool calls, blocked operations, and approvals — filterable by session and time range.
